programmes.show_description: This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
On the Gogglebox: Britain's Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Stafford's Rite of Passage, and Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time.
Bill demonstrates the influence of Cockney music on the classical greats and warns of a world ruled by insects, in his debut live stand-up show at the Bloomsbury Theatre, back in 1996.
Sun, sea and saving money? Yes please! Jasmine Harman reveals industry tricks to indulgent beach getaways for less, from the inside scoop on resorts to cutting costs of package trips. (Ep5)
The Dog House's long-term residents need new homes. Can inseparable Chihuahua trio Rio, Prince and Blue Boy, and double trouble George and Daisy, find their forever humans?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Fiona and John hope that their guests will embrace their green ethos at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man. Rivals Robbie and John are pleasantly surprised by the cleanliness.
Ex-demolition man Phil Routledge hopes to blow his guests away at the ultra-modern The Arches on the Isle of Man. But some of his high-tech gadgets leave them confused.
Can Robbie and John's traditional Blackpool B&B, The Trafford, impress their well-heeled rivals? The hosts try to sweeten the deal with a lesson in how to make Blackpool rock.
Empty-nester Julie Brogan welcomes her rivals to the grand Sheepy Lodge in Warwickshire, where she faces her worst fear: cooking poached eggs for her guests.
It's the final day of the competition, and the rival B&Bers meet to settle some scores and to find out how much they've been paid - and who's won the week.
Fiona and Michael kick off this week's competition at their homely country pub in Norfolk. But a problem with a shower makes a guest feel dour.
Carl and Liz host the week's second visit, at their coaching inn in Cambridgeshire, where converted stables and Highland cows are on the menu (not literally).
The B&Bers visit Barbara and Elizabeth's boutique B&B in Norfolk, where the hosts try to dance away some doubts about grout with an old-school Wurlitzer organ.
The week's final hosts, Helen and Carl, have their work cut out to impress at their Nottinghamshire glampsite after ruffling feathers with their feedback all week.
Cleanliness has been the buzz word this week, with pretty much all of the hosts unhappy about their less-than-sparkling scores. Have some of them been playing dirty?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Windsor, Henry Cole finds an Austrian moped that's missing just one key item: an engine. And Simon O'Brien's taken with a fibreglass suit of armour called Ralph.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Crewe, Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo meet rock 'n' roller Billy, who lives in a house that's frozen in time in the 1950s.
Leah Charles-King helps Sarah and Andy find the 'pawfect' place for their whole family - including their beloved dog Ruby - on Spain's southern Costa Blanca, for their budget of £160k.
Sarah and Ian met in Spain and now want a holiday home in south-east Lanzarote for themselves and their family to enjoy. Jean Johansson shows them five great finds.
Jane and James scour eastern Crete for an Aegean gem on a budget of £130k. Will a house packed full of charm or a villa with wrap-around views be the perfect place for them?
Lucy Alexander helps Tracy and Lee find a French holiday home in Lower Normandy, which they hope could one day become their permanent home, for their budget of £70k.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Maidenhead, Nilesh and Raakhee are put off by their shut-off kitchen. Can Kirstie deliver a love-it layout, or will Phil's alternative abodes lure them to list it?
This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
...Harold Fry: (2023) Rachel Joyce's poignant drama starring Jim Broadbent. A retired man walks from Devon to Berwick to save a former colleague from cancer, using nothing but faith.
The Butcher of Suburbia: The landmark series brings us the gripping inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman - and the killer's macabre efforts to conceal her death. (Ep1/2)
The Butcher of Suburbia: The suspect admits to the murder of a 74-year-old woman. But a deeply disturbing tale that shocks experienced detectives has only just begun. (Ep2/2)
This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
From open marriages to late-life romance, welcome to a night of secrets, surprises and second chances at the nation's most romantic restaurant.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Sue and Richard's beautiful home in Mapperley is no longer suitable since Richard had a cycling accident. Can Kirstie make it accessible? Or should they list it?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with young couple Wendy and Simon in Derbyshire and their oldest ever house-hunters, Margot and Henry, in Berkshire.
Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj throw themselves into the restoration of a derelict chapel in Pwllheli. But as they explore their new home, it seems they're not alone...
In South Wales, farm girl Claire treats her guests to a traditional, hearty Welsh spread and farm-themed games. Will her farm charm impress? Or will her night end up a mucky mess? (Ep1/5)
Fiona and Michael kick off this week's competition at their homely country pub in Norfolk. But a problem with a shower makes a guest feel dour.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: On the Costa Blanca, Abi and Darren plan a disco revolution. In France, will juggling a working business and renovations prove too much for restaurateurs Andrew and Richard?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programmes.show_description: Chris and Kay are searching for the perfect retirement home to share with their cats, but can't agree on where it should be. Will they pick Cardigan Bay in west Wales or Spain's Mar Menor?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Jasmine Harman revisits dad and daughter Steven and Stephanie in Charente. Laura Hamilton's with Neelesh and Angie, who bought an apartment in Estepona in 2019.
Swindon. International DJ Mark hosts a rave-themed evening, with an Ibiza-inspired menu including 'big fish little fishcakes' and 'Bora Bora Beach brownies'. (Ep1/5)
Swindon. Amateur dramatics fan Michaela plans a glamorous evening with a full band accompaniment for her guests to sing show tunes. There's just one problem: she's not a great cook. (Ep2/5)
Swindon. The joker of the group, financial advisor Scott, hopes to blow away the competition with a Wild West theme, including his unique style of country rapping. (Ep3/5)
Swindon. Sales manager Sally hosts a 'girls night in', with a cryptic menu that leaves her guests with no idea what they're about to eat. (Ep4/5)
Swindon. Alternative therapist Gill has big plans for an 'upstairs downstairs' Edwardian evening, featuring sophisticated food, followed by circle dancing to Bob Marley. (Ep5/5)
Dee and Joe have decided to swap Ireland for Portugal and make a permanent move to their favourite town of Albufeira. Jean Johansson shows them what their £155k can buy.
Kirstie and Phil catch up with cautious first-time buyers Sundeep and Rupreet in Harrow and postcode-picky Hilary and Rob, who set their hearts on Hitchin in Hertfordshire.
Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj throw themselves into the restoration of a derelict chapel in Pwllheli. But as they explore their new home, it seems they're not alone...

This time it's Corrie, The Stolen Girl, The Piano, Mickey Rourke on Celebrity Big Brother, savoury cocktails on Saturday Kitchen, the News on Katy Perry in space, and more.
From open marriages to late-life romance, welcome to a night of secrets, surprises and second chances at the nation's most romantic restaurant.

programmes.show_name: Rhod Gilbert: The Book of John
programmes.show_description: Raw, personal and brutally honest, award-winning comedian Rhod takes us on a no-nonsense journey into finding humour when you've hit rock-bottom. Recorded at the Wales Millennium Centre.
